skill.name: longbridge-intel
skill.description:
Market intelligence: strategy screener, popularity rankings, top movers with news correlation, quote anomalies, index/ETF constituent stocks, morning briefings, catalyst monitoring for watchlist, event-driven strategies, ETF fund flows, sector rotation, market microstructure, supply chain analysis, industry overviews, and ARK-style disruptive innovation analysis.
Triggers: "筛选", "策略筛选", "排行", "热度", "异动", "成分股", "晨报", "早报", "催化剂", "事件驱动", "ETF资金流", "板块轮动", "产业链", "行业概览", "颠覆式创新", "ARK", "篩選", "排行", "異動", "成分股", "晨報", "ETF資金流", "板塊輪動", "產業鏈", "screener", "rank", "anomaly", "constituent", "morning brief", "catalyst", "event strategy", "ETF flow", "ETF资金流", "ETF申赎", "ETF資金流", "etf flow", "资金申赎", "etf 资金", "sector rotation", "supply chain", "ARK", "disruptive innovation", "板块筛选", "行业筛选", "板塊篩選", "強勢板塊", "弱勢板塊", "top sectors", "催化劑", "事件驅動", "行業概覽", "顛覆式創新", "策略篩選", "熱度"
skill.summary: Prompt-only market intelligence hub: screening, ranking, anomaly detection, ETF/sector flow, microstructure, supply-chain, and thematic research via Longbridge CLI.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: All 308 static findings are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' matches are triple-backtick markdown code fences in documentation, not executable code. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' hits are financial-analysis terminology (e.g., 'block trades', 'hash' in the array sense) and ARK-methodology terms like 'Wright's Law' — not cryptography. The 'System reconnaissance' hits are market-research reconnaissance (screening/sector scans), not OS-level recon. The skill is prompt-only (no scripts/), read-only (risk_level: read_only), and requires no login. The single 'Hardcoded URL' in catalyst-radar.md:14 is an example command flag, not an exfiltration endpoint.

low_findings.title: External CLI invocations in prompt documentation
low_findings.description: Skill instructs the LLM to call `longbridge <subcommand>` commands. These are first-party CLI calls via the user's shell, not arbitrary code execution — they are documented CLI usage, not injection vectors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of SKILL.md confirms all 'commands' are longbridge CLI examples, not spawn/exec. The skill is prompt-only with no scripts/ directory.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL example in documentation
low_findings.description: A URL string appears in references/catalyst-radar.md as part of a CLI example flag value, not an active network call by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed context — the URL is embedded in a `--help` output example within a markdown code block, not an exfiltration destination.

content.user_title: Run Market Intelligence Screeners and Sector Analysis
content.value_statement: Screening stocks, tracking sector rotation, and reading ETF flows manually is slow and fragmented. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a unified Longbridge-backed intelligence hub for screeners, anomalies, catalysts, and thematic research.
actual_capabilities: Run strategy screeners with custom indicator filters (P/E, ROE, etc.) across HK, US, CN, and SG markets
Surface popularity rankings, top movers with correlated news, and quote anomalies in one briefing
Look up index and ETF constituent stocks, including AUM, expense ratio, and tracking-error data
Generate morning briefings covering overnight moves, watchlist catalysts, and upcoming events
Scan watchlist for catalysts across 7 dimensions: earnings, policy, capital flow, insider trades, and analyst upgrades
Analyze ETF fund flows, sector rotation signals, and supply-chain dependencies for thematic research
limitations: Read-only — does not place orders or modify portfolios; account-level actions require a separate skill
Output quality depends on the LLM correctly discovering `longbridge <subcommand> --help` flags at runtime
ARK-style disruptive innovation analysis uses a simplified 3-scenario model, not the full ARK research pipeline
Microstructure metrics (VPIN, Kyle lambda) are computed from available Longbridge data, which may not include all institutional flow detail

use_cases.title: Pre-market morning briefing
use_cases.description: Generate a daily pre-market summary covering overnight moves, watchlist catalysts, and today's earnings calendar.

use_cases.title: Thematic sector rotation scan
use_cases.description: Compare sector strength, ETF flows, and constituent stock momentum to identify rotation opportunities.

use_cases.title: Event-driven watchlist monitoring
use_cases.description: Track earnings, buybacks, management changes, and index inclusions across a watchlist with 7-dimension catalyst scoring.

prompt_templates.title: Run a basic value screener
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen HK stocks with P/E ratio between 8 and 20 and ROE above 10%. Show the top 20 results with their P/E, ROE, and market cap.

prompt_templates.title: Morning briefing for a watchlist
prompt_templates.prompt: Give me a morning brief for my watchlist: NVDA.US, 700.HK, 600519.SH. Cover overnight moves, any catalysts from the last 24 hours, and today's events.

prompt_templates.title: Sector rotation comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the current sector rotation between US tech, China EV, and HK financials using ETF fund flow data and sector strength. Which is showing the strongest momentum?

prompt_templates.title: ARK-style innovation analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Do an ARK-style disruptive innovation analysis for the humanoid-robotics theme: TAM sizing, Wright's Law cost curve, and a 3-scenario 5-year price target for the top 3 names.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert — thematic deep-dive with quantitative framework

output_examples.input: Screen HK stocks with P/E between 8 and 20 and ROE above 10%.
output: Overnight: NVDA +1.2% on AI chip news; 700.HK -0.4%; 600519.SH flat
Watchlist bias: cautious on HK tech, neutral on A-shares consumer

output_examples.input: Compare US tech, China EV, and HK financials sector rotation.
output: US tech: ETF inflow $1.2B (5d), top constituents all positive 1W
China EV: ETF inflow $0.4B (5d), mixed constituent performance, 2/5 up 5%+
HK financials: ETF outflow $0.3B (5d), broad-based weakness
Rotation signal: US tech leading, HK financials lagging

best_practices: Always run `longbridge <subcommand> --help` first to discover the current flags — the CLI evolves and the skill does not hard-code subcommand names.
Specify the market explicitly (HK, US, CN, SG) to avoid ambiguity when screening, since the same ticker format can mean different things across regions.
Combine the screener with the catalyst radar for a fuller picture — a stock passing a value filter is more interesting if it also has a near-term catalyst.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the 3-scenario ARK model as a price target guarantee — it is a framework for thinking about uncertainty, not a forecast.
Do not use microstructure metrics outside regular trading hours without disclosing that the data is a closing snapshot, not a live orderbook.
Do not recommend specific stocks to other users as financial advice — the skill is an analysis tool, not a financial advisor.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades or manage my account?
faq.answer: No. The skill is read-only (risk_level: read_only). It fetches market data and runs screeners, but does not place, modify, or cancel orders.

faq.question: Do I need to log in to use the longbridge-intel skill?
faq.answer: No. All CLI commands in this skill use public market data. No authentication is required, though the longbridge CLI must be installed on your machine.

faq.question: How is this different from the longbridge-quote or longbridge-news skills?
faq.answer: longbridge-quote fetches raw price data, and longbridge-news returns headlines. longbridge-intel is the intelligence layer on top: screeners, anomaly detection, sector rotation, catalyst scoring, and thematic research.

faq.question: What languages does the skill respond in?
faq.answer: Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English. The skill matches the language of your input, and the output table headers are always trilingual.

faq.question: Why do some prompts say 'run longbridge --help first'?
faq.answer: The longbridge CLI evolves. The skill avoids hard-coding subcommand names or flags so it does not go stale. The LLM discovers the right invocation at runtime via --help.

faq.question: Can I use this for backtesting or paper trading?
faq.answer: No. The skill is analysis-only. It does not have access to historical intraday data for backtesting strategies, nor does it integrate with a paper-trading account.
